response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain expertise (founder of an open-source Geo-Data platform, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second with asynchronous I/O at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, that systems work was in DLang rather than C++ or Rust, his hands-on C/C++ experience is brief and dates to 2014-2015, and recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. He lacks direct experience with routing pipelines or navigation guidance systems, making him a plausible but risky fit who would need to ramp up on the core language and domain.
strengths: Deep geospatial data experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and Linux/Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; primary systems language is DLang, not C++ or Rust
No direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, away from systems-level engineering
